the Copilot memory settings
the notebook where the conversation is stored
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Rename a chat Additional Reading: Revisit your Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat history Rationale: Renaming a conversation changes only the conversation title, so it is easier to identify later. It does not alter the content of the conversation, does not modify Copilot memory settings, and does not automatically move or change the notebook where the conversation is stored.

questions.question: You use Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat to work on multiple projects each month. You need to reopen a conversation from several weeks ago. Which two actions can you perform to locate the conversation? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
options: Search for keywords.
View Copilot memory.
Review the chat history.
Start a new chat and enter the same prompt.

questions.question: You regularly use Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat to research client proposals. A colleague asks you to review a conversation you had several weeks ago about a specific client. Where should you go to locate the conversation?
options: the chat history
Copilot memory
Microsoft 365 file search
a new chat
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Understand how to find previous conversations Additional Reading: Revisit your Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat history Rationale: The chat history in Copilot Chat displays previous conversations and enables you to reopen them. Copilot memory stores saved preferences and personalization details but does not contain full conversation threads. Microsoft 365 file search retrieves documents and files rather than Copilot Chat conversations. Starting a new chat creates a new conversation rather than retrieving an existing one.

questions.question: You are using Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat to plan a product launch. You need to keep an existing project conversation organized with related files and notes, so you can continue the conversation later. What should you do?
options: Rename the conversation.
Add the conversation to a notebook.
Share the conversation with your team.
Pin the conversation in the chat history.
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Add a conversation to a notebook Additional Reading: Revisit your Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat history Get started with Microsoft 365 Copilot Notebooks Rationale: Adding the conversation to a notebook organizes the existing chat with related materials, while preserving its content for continued work. Pinning the conversation keeps it visible in the chat history but does not group it with other resources. Renaming the conversation changes only its title and does not organize it. Sharing the conversation enables collaboration but does not structure it alongside related project content.

questions.question: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence. To enable a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent to reference the documents stored on a Microsoft SharePoint Online site when generating responses, you must configure the [Target_A] setting.
options: Capabilities
Instructions
Knowledge sources
Suggested prompts
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Configure an agent that has knowledge Additional Reading: Add knowledge sources to your declarative agent in Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Knowledge sources must be configured to allow the agent to reference documents stored in SharePoint or other organizational locations. Capabilities enable functional actions but do not provide access to content. Instructions define how the agent behaves or formats responses. Suggested prompts provide example user queries but do not connect the agent to enterprise data.

questions.question: You build a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent for the procurement team at your company. The agent must respond in bullet points and limit answers to three recommendations. During testing, the agent returns long paragraph responses. What should you configure?
options: Capabilities
Instructions
Knowledge sources
Suggested prompts
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Configure agent settings such as instructions, capabilities and suggested prompts Additional Reading: Write effective instructions for declarative agents Build agents by using Agent Builder in Microsoft 365 Copilot Build agents by using Agent Builder in Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Instructions control how the agent formats and structures its responses, including requirements, such as using bullet points and limiting the number of recommendations. Capabilities enable specific functional actions and do not control the response format. Knowledge sources establish which content the agent can reference, not how it presents information. Suggested prompts provide example user queries but do not enforce response behavior.

questions.question: You are building a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent for the legal team at your company. You need the agent to always respond in a formal tone and include a disclaimer at the end of every response. Which setting should you configure?
options: Capabilities
Instructions
Knowledge sources
Suggested prompts
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Configure agent settings such as instructions, capabilities and suggested prompts Additional Reading: Write effective instructions for declarative agents Build agents by using Agent Builder in Microsoft 365 Copilot Use natural language to describe your agent Rationale: Instructions define how the agent behaves and formats its responses, including tone and required elements, such as disclaimers. Capabilities enable specific functional actions the agent can perform. Knowledge sources connect the agent to organizational content. Suggested prompts provide example questions for users but do not control response behavior.

questions.question: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence. You can use [Target_A] to add and start using a prebuilt agent without creating a new one.
options: the Agent Store
Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat
Microsoft 365 Copilot Studio
the Agent Builder in Microsoft 365 Copilot
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Understand when to use Agent Store versus creating a new agent Additional Reading: Choose what type of agent to build Introducing the Agent Store: Build, publish, and discover agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: The Agent Store is used to browse, add, and use prebuilt agents without creating a new agent. The Agent Builder and Copilot Studio are used to create or customize agents, which involves configuration work. Copilot Chat provides conversational assistance but does not provide reusable prebuilt agents.

questions.question: You are creating a new agent in Microsoft 365 Copilot. You need to ensure that the agent can generate images in response to user prompts. Which field should you configure?
options: Capabilities
Description
Instructions
Knowledge
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Configure agent settings such as instructions, capabilities and suggested prompts Additional Reading: Add the image generator capability to your agent Rationale: Capabilities control which actions an agent can perform, including generating images, making this the correct choice. Description is used to summarize the agent’s purpose for users and does not affect functionality. Instructions guide how the agent should behave or respond but does not enable image generation. Knowledge defines the content sources the agent can reference and is unrelated to enabling generative features such as images.

questions.question: You are preparing for a meeting with a potential partner. You need Microsoft 365 Copilot to draft a first version of a partnership proposal in Microsoft Word, even though no related documents currently exist. What should you do?
options: Ask Copilot to suggest a proposal.
Upload meeting notes from a previous project.
Search for similar proposals in Microsoft Teams.
Describe the partnership goals and key requirements in a prompt.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Create a new document from a prompt Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Creating a new document from scratch by using Copilot begins by providing a prompt that describes the purpose, audience, and key requirements of the document. Searching for existing content, uploading unrelated materials, or waiting for automatic suggestions do not initiate document creation when no source documents exist.

questions.question: You are collaborating on the launch plan for a new product. You need to create a management summary based on an existing launch plan document by using Copilot in Microsoft Word. How should you use Copilot to draft the summary?
options: Rewrite the launch plan by using a more formal tone.
Summarize each section of the launch plan individually.
Copy the launch plan content into Word and summarize it.
Ask Copilot to create a new document by referencing the launch plan document.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Generate a document from an existing document Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Generating a management summary from an existing document requires Copilot to reference the original document while creating a new output tailored for leadership. Manually copying content, rewriting tone, and summarizing sections individually do not create a new document based on the existing one by using Copilot.

questions.question: You are working with a team to understand how Microsoft 365 Copilot can support daily work. You need to explain how Copilot helps users reuse information from one Microsoft 365 app while working in another. Which statement accurately describes this capability?
options: Copilot provides general AI responses that are NOT connected to your work data.
Copilot works within a single app and cannot reference information from other apps.
Copilot requires users to manually copy content between apps before the content can be reused.
Copilot can bring relevant information from emails, chats, and files into the app you are working in.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Move data and insights between Microsoft 365 apps Additional Reading: Work smarter with Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat Rationale: Copilot helps users move data and insights between apps by bringing relevant information from sources, such as emails, chats, and files, into the current app context. Copilot does not operate in isolation, does not require manual copying, and does not limit users to a single app when reusing work information.

questions.question: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence. With Copilot in Microsoft Word, you can create a new document by [Target_A].
options: generating a rewritten version of the document with a different structure
referencing an existing document as the starting point
summarizing the document into a shorter executive version
transforming the document into a different format, such as a report outline
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Generate a document from an existing document Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Copilot in Word can create a new document by referencing an existing document as the starting point, which enables content to be reused in a separate file. Rewriting, summarizing, or transforming the document changes the form of the same content rather than generating a new document based on an existing one.

questions.question: You are drafting a proposal in by using Copilot in Microsoft Word. Important background information is spread across emails in Microsoft Outlook and meeting notes in Microsoft Teams. You want Copilot to incorporate the relevant information into the proposal, while minimizing manual effort and duplication. What should you do?
options: Export the Outlook emails and Teams notes as files and reference them in Copilot.
Use Copilot in Word to incorporate insights from related Outlook emails and Teams meetings.
Use Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat to summarize the emails and meetings notes before drafting the proposal.
Paste key excerpts from the emails and meeting notes into the Word document, and then ask Copilot to refine them.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Move data and insights between Microsoft 365 apps Additional Reading: Introduction to Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Using Copilot directly in Word to incorporate insights from Outlook emails and Teams meetings enables information to move seamlessly across Microsoft 365 apps without manual copying, exporting, or context switching. Exporting files or pasting excerpts introduces unnecessary steps and duplication, while summarizing content separately in Copilot Chat adds an extra workflow that does not take full advantage of Copilot in-app, cross-app integration.

questions.question: You create an employee onboarding overview on a Microsoft 365 Copilot Page that includes information about company culture, core values, and onboarding goals. You need to reuse key information from the Copilot Page to create a Microsoft PowerPoint presentation. How should you use Copilot to do this?
options: Import the Copilot Page as a PDF into PowerPoint..
Copy the content from the Copilot Page to PowerPoint.
Rewrite the content in PowerPoint by using Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat.
Use the Copilot Page reference as an input for Copilot when creating the presentation.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Move data and insights between Microsoft 365 apps Additional Reading: Work smarter with Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat Introduction to Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Using a Copilot Page as a reference allows Copilot to reuse existing content across Microsoft 365 apps without manual copying or file conversion. Copying content, exporting files, and rewriting information introduces extra steps and increases the risk of losing or altering key insights.

questions.question: You use Microsoft 365 Copilot to create a business case document in Microsoft Word. You want Copilot to generate the document based on your requirements. What should you do first?
options: Copy and paste your related notes into Copilot.
Select the formatting options for the document.
Choose a document template for the business case.
Describe the business case you want Copilot to create in a prompt.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Create a new document from a prompt Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: To create a new document by using Microsoft 365 Copilot, start by describing what you want Copilot to generate in a prompt, which defines the purpose and scope of the document. Other references and files can be added later to refine or ground the output, but they are optional and not required to begin document creation. Choosing templates or formatting options also occurs after Copilot generates an initial draft.

questions.question: You are reviewing a detailed draft document created by using Microsoft 365 Copilot. You need to provide senior leaders with a short, executive-level overview that highlights the most important points in the document. What should you ask Copilot to do?
options: Summarize each section of the document in detail.
Convert the document into a slide-based presentation.
Create a concise management summary highlighting key outcomes.
Rewrite the document to match an executive communication style.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Generate a management summary based on a document Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Creating a concise management summary focuses on key outcomes and provides an executive-level overview suitable for senior leaders. Converting the document into slides changes the format, rewriting the document changes the purpose, and summarizing each section in detail produces content that is too granular for an executive summary.

questions.question: You use Microsoft 365 Copilot to create a document. You need to start the document by describing what you require Copilot to write. What should you use?
options: an existing document
meeting notes from Microsoft Teams
a prompt
a saved Copilot conversation
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Create a new document from a prompt Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Copilot can create a new document when you provide a prompt that describes the content you want to generate. Existing documents, saved conversations, and meeting notes can be used as references, but they are not required to create a new document from scratch.

questions.question: You capture meeting outcomes and notes on a Microsoft 365 Copilot Page edited by multiple members of a team. You need to create a Microsoft PowerPoint presentation that reflects the latest shared content. The solution must minimize a rework of the content. What should you do?
options: Use Copilot to generate a PowerPoint presentation directly from the Copilot Page.
Copy the Copilot Page content into PowerPoint and ask Copilot to format the slides.
Export the Copilot Page to Microsoft Word, and then use Copilot to generate slides.
Create a new presentation in PowerPoint by using Copilot Chat based on a general description of the meeting outcomes
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Generate a document from an existing document Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Creating the presentation directly from the Copilot Page by using Copilot ensures the latest collaboratively edited content is used and minimizes manual effort. The other options introduce unnecessary copying, step conversion, and content recreation, which increases effort and risks losing updates.

questions.question: You use Copilot in Microsoft Teams to help with meetings. During a transcribed meeting, you prompt Copilot to focus summaries on action items. After the meeting ends, you ask Copilot to summarize the meeting. How does Copilot use the provided instruction?
options: Copilot ignores the instruction once the meeting has ended.
Copilot requires the instructions to be restated after the meeting.
Copilot uses the instruction to emphasize action items in the summary.
Copilot applies the instruction only if the transcription ran for the entire meeting.
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how Copilot uses memory and instructions Additional Reading: Recap a Teams meeting Rationale: Copilot can use instructions provided during a meeting to guide generated follow-up content, such as meeting summaries. The other options incorrectly suggest that instructions are discarded after the meeting, depend on full transcription, or must always be repeated.

questions.question: You organize a Microsoft Teams meeting. You need to ensure that participants can interact with Copilot while the meeting is in progress. The solution must ensure that the participants cannot access Copilot or Copilot-generated content after the meeting ends. Which Copilot meeting option should you select?
options: During and after the meeting
Off
Only d uring the meeting
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how to use Copilot for meetings Additional Reading: Use Copilot in Microsoft Teams meetings Rationale: Selecting Only d uring the meeting enables participants to use Copilot while the meeting is in progress and prevents Copilot access after the meeting ends. Allowing Copilot during and after the meeting enables post-meeting access, and turning Copilot off prevents in-meeting use.

questions.question: You are using Microsoft 365 Copilot and must understand how Copilot personalizes responses over time. Which information is stored in Copilot memory?
options: chat history from previous conversations
saved memories and user instructions
temporary meeting transcripts
user prompts from the last 90 days
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how Copilot uses memory and instructions Additional Reading: Manage Copilot Memory in Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Copilot memory stores information that users explicitly save as memories along with user-defined instructions that guide how Copilot responds over time. Chat history, prompts, and meeting transcripts are retained separately and are not stored as part of Copilot memory.

questions.question: You are in a Microsoft Teams meeting where multiple topics are being discussed rapidly. Before the meeting moves on to a new agenda item, you plan to use Copilot to synthesize the decisions made so far and any unresolved questions. What should you ask Copilot to do?
options: Summarize the meeting after it ends.
Schedule a follow-up meeting in Microsoft Outlook.
Provide a verbatim transcript of the meeting conversation.
Generate a list of open questions and decisions discussed so far.
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how to use Copilot for meetings Additional Reading: Manage Copilot for Teams meetings and events Use Copilot in Microsoft Teams meetings Frequently asked questions about Copilot in Microsoft Teams Rationale: Copilot can be used during a Teams meeting to surface synthesized insights, such as decisions made and unresolved questions, which helps participants align before moving forward. A transcript provides raw conversation without synthesis, and post-meeting summaries or follow-up scheduling do not support real-time collaboration during the meeting.

questions.question: You need Copilot to generate a meeting summary and a list of action items after a Microsoft Teams meeting ends. What should you enable during the meeting?
options: live captions
meeting recording
meeting transcription
Together mode

questions.question: You have two Microsoft Teams meetings that were recorded and transcribed. You need to generate a written list of decisions and action items based only on what was discussed, without manually reviewing the recordings. What should you do in Teams?
options: Search the meeting chat for keywords, such as decision and action item.
Turn on Together mode and ask Copilot to generate decisions and action items.
Use Copilot in each meeting recap to summarize the discussion and identify decisions and action items.
Export the attendance report and ask Copilot to infer decisions and action items from the attendee list.
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how to use Copilot for meetings Additional Reading: Streamline meeting preparation and follow ups with Copilot Rationale: Using Copilot in each meeting to summarize the discussion and identify decisions and action items generates structured output directly from the meeting transcript without manually reviewing the recordings. Exporting the attendance report does not include meeting content and cannot provide decisions or action items. Searching the meeting chat can miss a verbal discussion that was not typed. Turning on Together mode only changes the meeting layout and does not affect Copilot summarization capabilities.

questions.question: You use Copilot in Microsoft Outlook to summarize a negotiation with a vendor. The key negotiation details were discussed across multiple replies within one email conversation. What should you reference in the prompt to ensure that Copilot includes the complete discussion?
options: the entire mailbox
the specific email conversation
the vendor contact
your recent Outlook messages
questions.note: Objective: 2.1 Create and manage prompts in Microsoft 365 Copilot What This Item Tests: Select appropriate resources to reference in a prompt Additional Reading: Chat with Copilot in Outlook Rationale: The specific email conversation is correct because it contains all the replies and ensures that Copilot uses the full negotiation context. Referencing the entire mailbox expands the scope beyond the relevant discussion. Referencing the vendor contact does not guarantee the inclusion of the complete conversation history. Using recent Outlook messages might omit earlier replies in the negotiation thread.

questions.question: You use Microsoft 365 Copilot to draft a project update for senior management. You need to ensure that the prompt generates a clear and useful response. Which two elements should you include in the prompt? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: a clear description of the task to be completed
a specific audience and an expected output format
background information about how long the project has existed
instructions to make the response as detailed as possible
the names of the team members involved in the project
questions.note: Objective: 2.1 Create and manage prompts in Microsoft 365 Copilot What This Item Tests: Understand how to create an effective prompt Additional Reading: Craft effective prompts for Microsoft 365 Copilot Write effective instructions for declarative agents Rationale: A clear description of the task ensures that Copilot understands the objective of the request. A specific audience and expected output format guide tone, structure, and relevance, which improves response quality. Background information about project duration might provide context, but it is not a core structural element of an effective prompt. Asking for maximum detail can reduce clarity if not paired with a defined goal or format. Listing team members does not inherently improve prompt effectiveness unless directly tied to the task.

questions.question: In Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, you create a prompt that generates a structured quarterly report. You need the prompt to be available in your saved prompts list so you can reuse it later. What does saving the prompt enable?
options: read-only protection of the prompt
organization-wide access to the prompt
availability of the prompt from your saved prompts list
automatic execution of the prompt at scheduled intervals
questions.note: Objective: 2.1 Create and manage prompts in Microsoft 365 Copilot What This Item Tests: Save a prompt Additional Reading: Understand Prompt Gallery in Copilot Rationale: Availability of the prompt in your saved prompts list is the correct answer because saving a prompt stores it for future reuse within Copilot. Automatic execution at scheduled intervals requires scheduling the prompt, which is a separate feature. Organization-wide access requires sharing the prompt with other users and does not occur automatically when saving. Read-only protection is not a function of saving; saved prompts can still be edited by the user who saved them.

questions.question: You are using Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat to plan a project. You need to keep an existing conversation grouped with related project materials, so you can continue working on the project over time. What should you do?
options: Pin the chat.
Rename the chat.
Start a new chat.
Add the conversation to a notebook.
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Add a conversation to a notebook Additional Reading: Get started with Microsoft 365 Copilot Notebooks Rationale: Adding the conversation to a notebook groups it with related materials and enables continued work in context. Pinning the chat keeps it visible in the chat history but does not organize it with other project content. Renaming the chat changes only its title. Starting a new chat creates a separate conversation rather than organizing an existing one.

questions.question: You delete a conversation from Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat. What occurs to the conversation?
options: The conversation is removed only from any notebooks.
The conversation title is reset to its original name.
The conversation is no longer visible in the chat history.
The conversation remains in the chat history but is marked as inactive.
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Delete a chat Additional Reading: Revisit your Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat history Rationale: Deleting a conversation removes it from the chat history in Copilot Chat, so the conversation is no longer visible. This action is not limited to notebooks, and the conversation is not retained in an inactive state. Deleting the conversation does not reset or modify its title; instead, it removes the conversation entirely from view.

questions.question: You are using Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat to research marketing ideas for a new product launch. After the chat, you need the conversation title to clearly reflect the topic, so you can easily identify the conversation later. What should you do?
options: Rename the chat from the conversation settings.
Export the chat and reload it with a new file name.
Create a new chat and copy the previous responses to it.
Delete the existing chat and start a new chat that has the new title.
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Rename a chat Additional Reading: Revisit your Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat history Rationale: Renaming the chat from the conversation options enables you to update the conversation title without losing the existing chat history, which supports the better organization and retrieval of prior discussions. Creating a new chat requires duplicating content and does not modify the original conversation. Deleting the existing chat removes the conversation entirely. Exporting and reloading the chat changes file artifacts but does not rename the Copilot conversation within the chat interface.

questions.question: You are configuring the following instruction for a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent that drafts internal status reports: “Provide a detailed explanation of all project activities and keep the response under 100 words.” During testing, responses are inconsistent in length and detail. What is the most likely cause of the issue?
options: The agent must include suggested prompts.
The Capabilities setting is not configured.
The agent requires additional knowledge sources.
The agent instruction contains conflicting guidance.
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Configure agent settings such as instructions, capabilities and suggested prompts Additional Reading: Write effective instructions for declarative agents Rationale: The instruction includes conflicting guidance by requiring both a detailed explanation of all activities and a strict word limit, which can lead to inconsistent responses. Knowledge sources affect content access but do not resolve structural conflicts. Suggested prompts do not control how the agent formats responses. Capabilities enable specific functional actions and are unrelated to instruction clarity.

questions.question: You are configuring a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent for a department at your company. When the users open the agent, you want them to see example questions that they can ask the agent. Which setting should you configure?
options: Capabilities
Instructions
Knowledge
Suggested prompts
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Configure agent settings such as instructions, capabilities and suggested prompts Additional Reading: Build agents by using Agent Builder in Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Suggested prompts display example questions that users can select when interacting with the agent. Capabilities enable specific actions the agent can perform, such as generating images or performing tasks. Instructions define how the agent should behave or respond. Knowledge connects the agent to organizational content sources.

questions.question: You are building a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent for the finance team at your company. The agent must answer questions by referencing internal budgeting documents on a Microsoft SharePoint Online site. You need to ensure that the agent uses the documents to generate responses. What should you do?
options: Add suggested prompts related to budgeting.
Add the SharePoint site as a knowledge source.
Add instructions that describe the budgeting process.
Update the agent description to mention budgeting support.

questions.question: You build a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent for a team named Sales West. You need to ensure that only the users on Sales West can use the agent during a pilot. What should you do?
options: Share the agent link in a Microsoft Teams channel.
Create a new agent specifically for each Sales West user.
Select a security group that contains the Sales West users.
Add the Sales West users individually by using Manage Sharing.
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Share an agent with team members Additional Reading: Share and manage agents

questions.question: You use Copilot in Microsoft Excel to analyze a worksheet that contains quarterly sales figures. When you ask Copilot to explain trends in the data, the response focuses only on the numbers in the worksheet and does NOT reference industry benchmarks or market trends from the web. Why did Copilot generate this type of response?
options: Copilot cannot use web data when working in Excel.
Copilot restricts responses to numeric content only.
Copilot prioritized the data in the current workbook.
Copilot requires explicit permission to access web data.
questions.note: Objective: 1.1 Understand generative AI capabilities across Microsoft 365 experiences What This Item Tests: Understand how the context, like your work files, web data, or the app you’re using, can affect Copilot responses Additional Reading: Application card: Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Copilot uses the context of the app and the content currently in focus, such as an Excel worksheet, to ground its responses, which is why the response is based on the workbook’s data rather than external information. Copilot can use web data when appropriate, is not limited to numeric-only responses, and does not require special permission to reference web data in this scenario.

questions.question: You are comparing Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat and Microsoft 365 Copilot. Which statement accurately describes a key difference between the two experiences?
options: Copilot Chat includes in-app editing features, while Copilot does NOT.
Copilot Chat can summarize Microsoft 365 files by default, while Copilot cannot.
Copilot Chat is available only on the web, while Copilot is available only in Microsoft Teams.
Copilot Chat provides a general-purpose AI chat experience, while Copilot uses Work IQ to ground responses in your organization’s Microsoft 365 data.
questions.note: Objective: 1.1 Understand generative AI capabilities across Microsoft 365 experiences What This Item Tests: Understand the differences in features and capabilities of the Copilot experience in various Microsoft 365 apps Additional Reading: Overview of Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat Microsoft 365 Copilot overview Rationale: Copilot Chat is designed as a general-purpose conversational AI experience, while Copilot is integrated into Microsoft 365 apps and grounded in organizational data, such as files, emails, meetings, and chats, which are subject to user permissions. The other options describe availability or capabilities that do not reflect how the two experiences differ.

questions.question: You need to understand how Microsoft 365 Copilot protects organizational data. Which statement accurately describes how Copilot handles organizational data?
options: Copilot restricts access to data by using sensitivity labels.
Copilot limits access to data based on Conditional Access policies.
Copilot requires administrators to manually approve each data request.
Copilot uses organizational data only within the tenant and does NOT use the data to train foundation models.
questions.note: Objective: 1.1 Understand generative AI capabilities across Microsoft 365 experiences What This Item Tests: Understand how Copilot works to keep your organization’s information private and secure Additional Reading: Security for Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Copilot processes organizational data within the tenant boundary and does not use customer data to train foundation models, which helps protect organizational information. Conditional Access and sensitivity labels address access and protection controls but do not describe how Copilot handles data for model training, and the manual approval of data requests is not part of the Copilot security model.

questions.question: A company plans to implement generative AI tools and wants to ensure that sensitive business and personal data is handled responsibly. You need to align the implementation with Microsoft responsible AI principles. Which responsible AI principle should you prioritize?
options: accountability
fairness
privacy and security
transparency
questions.note: Objective: 1.2 Identify responsible AI and data protection practices What This Item Tests: Recognize and mitigate risks to sensitive data Additional Reading: Responsible AI at Microsoft Rationale: The privacy and security principle focuses on protecting sensitive data, preventing unauthorized access, and ensuring compliance with data protection requirements, which directly addresses the risk of data exposure when using AI systems. Fairness and transparency address bias and explainability rather than data protection, and accountability relates to governance and oversight rather than safeguarding sensitive information.

questions.question: You are reviewing a document generated by using Microsoft 365 Copilot. The document includes references to external sources, but you are unsure whether the references accurately support the content. Which responsible AI practice should you follow?
options: Add more references by prompting Copilot again.
Remove the references to avoid potential inaccuracies.
Trust the references because Copilot generates them automatically.
Manually verify the referenced sources before relying on the content.
questions.note: Objective: 1.2 Identify responsible AI and data protection practices What This Item Tests: Select verification steps appropriate to the task, including citation checks and human review Additional Reading: Responsible AI at Microsoft Rationale: Responsible use of AI-generated content requires verifying referenced sources to ensure that they accurately support the information presented. Automatically trusting references, removing them without verification, and adding more references do not address the risk of hallucinated or incorrect citations.

questions.question: You use Microsoft 365 Copilot to draft a report that includes sensitive information. What should you expect when data protection policies are applied to the prompt?
options: Copilot stores sensitive prompt data for future reuse.
Copilot can generate responses only from the data that you can access.
Copilot ignores data protection policies when generating detailed reports.
Copilot automatically expands results beyond your data access permissions to improve accuracy.

questions.question: You have an existing document that contains detailed background information. You need to use Microsoft 365 Copilot to create a new document that reuses this information but is written for a different audience. What should you ask Copilot to do?
options: Summarize the existing document.
Create an outline based on the existing document.
Edit the existing document to change its tone and wording.
Generate a new document by using the existing document as a reference.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Generate a document from an existing document Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Generating a new document from an existing one requires using the original document as a reference, while creating a separate file tailored to a new audience. Summarizing the document reduces content rather than producing a new document, editing tone modifies the same document, and creating an outline prepares structure but does not result in a new document.

questions.question: You use Microsoft 365 Copilot to summarize a detailed project status report. Senior leaders need a brief update that focuses on risks, progress, and next steps, without operational details. What should you ask Copilot to do?
options: Generate a management summary.
Convert the report into slides.
Summarize each section of the report.
Rewrite the report by using an executive tone.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Generate a management summary based on a document Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: A management summary provides a concise, executive-level overview that highlights risks, progress, and next steps without including operational details. Converting the content into slides changes the format, rewriting the report alters tone without reducing details, and summarizing each section results in more information than the senior leaders need.

questions.question: You are reviewing a long business report in Microsoft Word. You need to provide senior leaders with a concise overview of the report that focuses on key decisions and outcomes, without sharing the full document. What should you do?
options: Use Copilot to translate the document.
Rewrite the document using a formal tone.
Copy sections of the document into Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat.
Ask Copilot to summarize the document for an executive audience.
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Generate a management summary based on a document Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Using Copilot to summarize the document for an executive audience enables you to generate a concise overview that focuses on key decisions and outcomes without rewriting or translating the content. Copying sections into Copilot Chat is unnecessary and inefficient, rewriting the document changes its purpose rather than summarizing it, and translation does not address the need for an executive-level summary.

questions.question: After a meeting, you need multiple team members to collaboratively edit and refine AI-generated notes in a shared workspace. Which Copilot capability supports this scenario?
options: Copilot meeting recap
Copilot prompts saved in a notebook
Microsoft 365 Copilot Pages
Microsoft 365Copilot Chat
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how to use Copilot for meetings Additional Reading: Get started with Microsoft 365 Copilot Pages Rationale: Copilot Pages provides a shared workspace where multiple users can collaboratively edit, organize, and refine AI-generated content after a meeting. Copilot Chat, meeting recaps, and saved prompts do not provide a persistent, shared editing surface for collaborative refinement.

questions.question: Which statement accurately describes how Copilot answers questions during a Microsoft Teams meeting?
options: Copilot references transcripts from other meetings in the tenant.
Copilot summarizes recent Teams chats and uses them to answer meeting questions.
Copilot answers questions based on the live meeting transcript and meeting context.
Copilot pulls the user’s emails, chats, and documents from Microsoft Graph during the meeting.

questions.question: You want meeting participants to use Microsoft 365 Copilot effectively for meeting follow-up tasks, such as asking questions and reviewing AI-generated insights, after a Microsoft Teams meeting ends. Which condition should be met to support this scenario?
options: Copilot must be allowed during and after the meeting.
End-to-end encryption must be enabled for the meeting.
External participants must be removed from the meeting.
The meeting must be scheduled by using Microsoft Outlook.
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how to use Copilot for meetings Additional Reading: Use Copilot in Microsoft Teams meetings Rationale: For Copilot to support meeting follow-up interactions after a meeting ends, it must be available both during and after the meeting so that Copilot context is retained. The encryption settings, participant composition, and scheduling method do not ensure that Copilot can be used for post-meeting follow-up.

questions.question: Which statement accurately describes how Microsoft 365 Copilot uses memory and instructions?
options: Copilot requires meeting transcripts to be enabled before it can remember information.
Copilot only uses information from the current conversation and cannot be personalized.
Copilot automatically remembers all chat content and permanently applies it to future responses.
Copilot can use previous chat context, and users can save information as memories and set instructions to personalize responses.
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how Copilot uses memory and instructions Additional Reading: Copilot personalization and memory Rationale: Copilot can reference prior chat context and enables users to explicitly save information as memories and define instructions that guide how responses are generated over time. Copilot does not automatically retain all chat content permanently, personalization is supported beyond the current conversation, and meeting transcripts are not a requirement for Copilot memory or instructions.

questions.question: You join a Microsoft Teams project review meeting after the meeting has already started. Microsoft 365 Copilot is enabled for the meeting. You need to understand quickly what has been discussed so far without interrupting the conversation. What should you ask Copilot to do?
options: Create action items after the meeting ends.
Generate a summary of the discussion so far.
Draft a follow-up email in Microsoft Outlook.
Start a real-time transcription of the meeting.
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how to use Copilot for meetings Additional Reading: Use Copilot in Microsoft Teams meetings Enhance meeting productivity with Copilot in Microsoft Teams Rationale: Copilot can generate an in-meeting summary that highlights key points discussed up to that moment, enabling a participant who joins late to quickly gain context without disrupting the meeting. A Transcription records spoken content but does not synthesize it, and post-meeting actions, such as action items or follow-up emails, do not address the immediate need during the meeting.

questions.question: You use Microsoft 365 Copilot to analyze customer feedback stored in a document. You have the following current prompt: “Analyze this customer feedback.” You need management to quickly understand the most significant issues and which actions the team should take next. Which revised prompt best achieves the goal? More than one answer choice may achieve the goal. Select the BEST answer.
options: Analyze this customer feedback and list actions mentioned in the comments.
Analyze this customer feedback in detail and suggest actions with supporting examples.
Analyze this customer feedback and provide a detailed summary of comments and suggested next steps.
Analyze this customer feedback and identify the top three recurring themes and two recommended actions for each.

questions.question: In Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, you create a prompt that generates a monthly financial summary. You need Copilot to run the prompt automatically at the beginning of each month. What should you do first?
options: Save the prompt.
Turn on the Web content toggle.
Share the prompt with your team.
Add the conversation to a notebook.
questions.note: Objective: 2.1 Create and manage prompts in Microsoft 365 Copilot What This Item Tests: Schedule a prompt Additional Reading: How to save prompts Rationale: Save the prompt is correct because a prompt must be saved before it can be scheduled for recurring execution. Adding the conversation to a notebook organizes the discussion but does not enable scheduling. Sharing the prompt enables others to reuse it but is not required for scheduling. Turning on the Web content toggle controls grounding behavior and is unrelated to scheduling.

questions.question: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence. With Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, you can group an existing conversation with related materials by using [Target_A].
options: an agent
the chat history
a notebook
a prompt
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Add a conversation to a notebook Additional Reading: Revisit your Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat history Get started with Microsoft 365 Copilot Notebooks Rationale: A notebook enables you to group an existing Copilot Chat conversation with related materials for continued work. An agent provides task-specific assistance but does not organize conversations. The chat history displays previous conversations but does not group them with additional materials. A prompt is a request entered into Copilot and does not organize conversations.

questions.question: You build a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent. You need a small group of users to test the agent in Microsoft Teams before the agent is released to the entire organization. What should you do?
options: Share the agent.
Sideload the agent.
Submit the agent to the organization catalog.
Publish the agent to the Microsoft commercial marketplace.
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Share an agent with team members Additional Reading: Share an agent Sideload agents at your organization Rationale: Sideloading the agent installs it in Teams for limited testing before broader deployment. Sharing the agent grants access permissions but does not make it available in Teams. Publishing to the Microsoft commercial marketplace distributes the agent externally. Submitting the agent to the organization catalog prepares it for wider internal availability rather than restricted pilot testing.

questions.question: Your company wants to provide employees with an agent that helps generate marketing campaign ideas. A prebuilt solution with this functionality is already available. You need to make this functionality available to the employees. The solution must minimize setup effort. What should you use?
options: the Agent Store
Microsoft 365 Copilot Studio
the Microsoft commercial marketplace
the Agent Builder in Microsoft 365 Copilot
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Understand when to use Agent Store versus creating a new agent Additional Reading: Choose what type of agent to build Introducing the Agent Store: Build, publish, and discover agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: The Agent Store is used to discover and add prebuilt agents that are already available for organizations, making it the appropriate choice when a suitable solution exists and minimal setup is required. The Agent Builder and Copilot Studio are used to create or customize agents, which involves additional configuration effort. The Microsoft commercial marketplace is intended for publishing solutions externally rather than installing existing agents for internal use.

questions.question: When you ask Microsoft 365 Copilot to summarize a project in Microsoft Word, you receive a response that is based on an internal project document but does not include information from public websites. Why did Copilot generate this type of response?
options: Copilot restricts responses to the files you created.
Copilot automatically excludes web data for all prompts.
Copilot only uses documents stored in Microsoft SharePoint Online.
Copilot prioritized content from the current app and your work files.
questions.note: Objective: 1.1 Understand generative AI capabilities across Microsoft 365 experiences What This Item Tests: Understand how the context, like your work files, web data, or the app you’re using, can affect Copilot responses Additional Reading: Microsoft 365 Copilot overview Rationale: Copilot uses contextual signals, such as the app you are working in and relevant work files, to ground its responses, which explains why the summary relied on an internal document in Word. Web data can be included depending on the experience and prompt, so it is not automatically excluded. Copilot is not limited to SharePoint-only content, nor does it restrict responses to files created by the current user.

questions.question: A company uses Microsoft 365 Copilot to help employees quickly ask questions and receive one-time responses during their daily work. The interactions do NOT need to follow predefined instructions, be reused, or use curated organizational knowledge. You need to choose the appropriate Copilot experience. What should you choose?
options: an agent experience
a role-based Copilot experience
the Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat experience
the Copilot in Microsoft 365 apps experience
questions.note: Objective: 1.1 Understand generative AI capabilities across Microsoft 365 experiences What This Item Tests: Understand the difference between a chat experience and an agent experience Additional Reading: Which Copilot is right for me or my organization? Overview of Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat Rationale: A Copilot Chat experience supports ad hoc, conversational interactions that provide one-time responses without persistent instructions or reusable behavior. Agent experiences are designed for repeatable, instruction-based interactions, Copilot in Microsoft 365 apps focuses on in-context productivity assistance, and role-based Copilot experiences are tailored to specific job functions.

questions.question: A company is implementing Microsoft 365 Copilot. Users need to ask questions, receive answers, and occasionally generate content based on organizational data. The interaction does not require executing tasks or automating business processes. You need to choose the appropriate type of Copilot interaction. What should you choose?
options: a chat-based Copilot experience
an agent that performs tasks on behalf of users
a custom Copilot Studio agent with multi-step workflows
an automated workflow triggered by Microsoft Power Automate
questions.note: Objective: 1.1 Understand generative AI capabilities across Microsoft 365 experiences What This Item Tests: Understand the difference between a chat experience and an agent experience Additional Reading: Agent in-app experience Overview of Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat Which Copilot is right for me or my organization? Rationale: A chat-based Copilot experience is appropriate for ad hoc interactions such as asking questions, generating content, and retrieving information without executing tasks; Agents are designed to perform actions and automate processes on behalf of users; Custom Copilot Studio agents and automated workflows introduce unnecessary complexity when task execution is not required.

questions.question: A company plans to create a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent that provides writing and presentation coaching based on organizational standards. The agent uses Microsoft SharePoint Online as a knowledge source and interacts with external business systems. You need to enable the agent to perform actions outside of Microsoft 365. Which capability should you add to the agent?
options: an adaptive card
a Copilot connector
a custom API action
an image generator
questions.note: Objective: 1.1 Understand generative AI capabilities across Microsoft 365 experiences What This Item Tests: Understand the use case for creating your own agent Additional Reading: Agent Builder in Microsoft 365 Copilot Build from a template Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: A custom API action enables a Copilot agent to call external services and perform actions beyond retrieving knowledge, which is required when the agent must interact with business systems outside of Microsoft 365. Copilot connectors are used to ingest external data rather than execute actions, image generation does not support system interaction, and adaptive cards control how information is displayed rather than extending agent functionality.

questions.question: A company uses Microsoft 365 Copilot. You need to reduce the risk of prompt injection attacks that can influence Copilot responses. Which two features help mitigate prompt injection attacks? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: Copilot encrypts all Microsoft 365 data at rest.
Copilot isolates the tenant data of Microsoft SharePoint Online.
Copilot applies grounding and filtering to prompts and responses.
Copilot enables users to review and edit generated content before use.
Copilot supports Microsoft Purview data loss prevention (DLP) policies.
questions.note: Objective: 1.2 Identify responsible AI and data protection practices What This Item Tests: Recognize and mitigate risks to sensitive data Additional Reading: Microsoft 365 Copilot and privacy controls for connected experiences Apply responsible AI principles Security for Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Copilot mitigates prompt injection by grounding responses in trusted organizational data and applying filters to reduce the impact of malicious or misleading prompts. Copilot also keeps users in the loop, so they can review and modify outputs before acting on them. Encryption, tenant isolation, and DLP policies protect sensitive data but do not directly address how prompt injection influences model behavior or responses.

questions.question: You are starting to write a new Microsoft Word document by using Microsoft 365 Copilot. You need Copilot to generate an initial draft that matches a specific audience, purpose, and tone, even though no reference documents exist. Which information is most important to include in the prompt?
options: the audience, goal, and tone of the document
the file location where the document will be saved
the length of time the document will be edited
the Microsoft 365 app where the document will be shared

questions.question: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence. You can use Microsoft 365 Copilot to [Target_A] when senior leaders need a brief overview of an existing document.
options: generate a management summary
improve a document’s tone
restructure a document
translate a document
questions.note: Objective: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Generate a management summary based on a document Additional Reading: Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Copilot can generate a management summary to provide senior leaders with a concise overview of key information from a document. Improving tone, restructuring content, and translating the document address different needs and do not produce a leadership-focused summary.

questions.question: You are using Copilot in Microsoft Word to create a new document. You want Copilot to generate the document by using content from an existing document. Which Copilot feature should you use?
options: Chat
Draft
Loop
Summarize
questions.note: Objective: 3.1 Draft business documents and communications What This Item Tests: Create a new document from a prompt Additional Reading: Draft and add content with Copilot in Word Create and draft with Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: The Draft feature in Microsoft 365 Copilot enables you to create a new document by referencing and reusing content from an existing document. Chat provides conversational responses, Loop is used for collaborative components rather than document drafting and Summarize condenses content instead of generating a new document.

questions.question: A company starts using Microsoft 365 Copilot Pages to collaborate on meeting outcomes across multiple teams. What is a key benefit of using Copilot Pages in this scenario?
options: automatically enforces data governance policies across Microsoft 365 tenants
defines organizational goals, use cases, and success metrics for AI adoption
Enables multiple users to collaboratively refine and update AI-generated content in one shared space
prepares an organization for a phased rollout of Microsoft 365 Copilot
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how to use Copilot Pages for collaboration Additional Reading: Collaborate with your team on a Microsoft 365 Copilot Page Rationale: Copilot Pages provide a shared, collaborative workspace where multiple users can review, edit, and build on AI-generated content together, making them well suited for coordinating meeting outcomes and shared work. The other options relate to governance, adoption planning, or rollout strategies, which are not functions of Copilot Pages and do not describe how they are used for collaboration.

questions.question: A user named User1 organizes three Microsoft Teams meetings for a project named Project1. Each meeting has a recording and transcript available in Teams. You need a written list of only the action items from the meetings. You cannot manually review the recordings or the transcripts. Which request should you make to Copilot?
options: Explain the Project1 meeting notes by using Copilot in Microsoft PowerPoint.
Find the last three Project1 meeting recordings and generate summaries for each recording.
Find meetings organized by User1 and summarize the action items from the last three meetings.
Create a new Teams meeting that includes User1 and generate a list of action items for Project1.
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how to use Copilot for meetings Additional Reading: Use Copilot to review past meetings Use Copilot in Microsoft Teams meetings Streamline meeting preparation and follow ups with Copilot Rationale: Asking Copilot to find meetings organized by User1 and summarize action items leverages meeting transcripts and recordings to extract the required information without manual review; Creating a new meeting does not retrieve or summarize past meeting content; Using Copilot in PowerPoint does not access Teams meeting transcripts or recordings; Summarizing recordings without filtering for meetings organized by User1 or focusing specifically on action items does not meet the requirement.

questions.question: In Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, you create a prompt that generates a weekly project status summary from a shared Microsoft Excel file. You need Copilot to automatically generate and send the updated summary to you every Friday without manually running the prompt. What should you configure?
options: a shared prompt for your team
a recurring runtime for the prompt
a saved prompt in Copilot Prompt Gallery
a summary notification in Microsoft Outlook
questions.note: Objective: 2.1 Create and manage prompts in Microsoft 365 Copilot What This Item Tests: Schedule a prompt Additional Reading: Schedule Copilot prompts Rationale: A recurring runtime for the prompt is correct because scheduling enables Copilot to run the prompt automatically at defined intervals without manual interaction. Saving the prompt makes it reusable but does not cause it to run automatically. Sharing the prompt enables others to use it but does not automate execution. A summary notification in Outlook does not trigger Copilot to generate content on a schedule.

questions.question: You build a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent and attempt to share the agent with a team at your company. The Share option is unavailable. You confirm that the agent is configured correctly. You need to identify why you cannot share the agent. What is the most likely cause of the issue?
options: The agent does not include suggested prompts.
The agent has no knowledge sources configured.
Agent sharing is restricted at the organization level.
The agent must first be published to the Microsoft commercial marketplace.
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Share an agent with team members Additional Reading: Share an agent Agents admin guide for Microsoft 365s Rationale: Agent sharing can be restricted by organizational policies configured by administrators, which can prevent users from sharing agents. Suggested prompts are optional and do not affect sharing capabilities. Knowledge sources are not required to enable sharing. Publishing to the Microsoft commercial marketplace is for external distribution and is not required for internal sharing.

questions.question: You are using Microsoft 365 Copilot Pages to collaborate with others on meeting notes and ideas. You need to reuse the content outside of Copilot Pages. Which two actions should you perform? Each correct answer presents part of the solution.
options: Convert the Copilot Page into a Microsoft Word document.
Generate real-time Copilot responses inside the Copilot Page.
Restrict the Copilot Page so that only the organizer can edit it.
Convert the Copilot Page into a Microsoft PowerPoint presentation.
Sync the Copilot Page automatically to a Microsoft OneNote notebook.
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how to use Copilot Pages for collaboration Additional Reading: Get started with Microsoft 365 Copilot Pages Rationale: Copilot Pages content can be reused by converting it into Word documents or PowerPoint presentations, so that collaborative work can be shared in common Microsoft 365 formats. Copilot Pages does not function as a real-time chat interface, does not automatically enforce organizer-only editing, and does not sync content directly to OneNote.

questions.question: You are using Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat to work on several projects. You need to change the title of the current conversation, so it is easier to identify later. What should you do?
options: Start a new chat.
Edit the conversation name.
Remove the conversation from the chat history.
Delete the conversation and start a new one with the desired name.
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Rename a chat Additional Reading: Revisit your Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat history Rationale: Editing the conversation name enables you to change the title without affecting the conversation content. Deleting the conversation removes it entirely rather than renaming it. Removing the conversation from the chat history deletes it instead of changing its name. Starting a new chat creates a separate conversation and does not modify the existing one.

questions.question: You and several team members are building an agent by using Agent Builder in Microsoft 365. You need to allow the team members to access and test the agent before the agent is broadly released. What should you do?
options: Share a link to the agent in a Microsoft Teams channel.
Use Manage Sharing in the Agent Builder to grant access.
Publish the agent to the Microsoft commercial marketplace.
Upload the agent files to a Microsoft SharePoint Online document library.
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Share an agent with team members Additional Reading: Share and manage agents Rationale: Using Manage Sharing in the Agent Builder is required to grant users access to the agent. Publishing to the Microsoft commercial marketplace distributes the agent externally rather than enabling internal team collaboration. Sharing a link in Teams does not grant permissions unless access is configured. Uploading files to SharePoint does not control access to the agent itself.

questions.question: You have a Microsoft 365 Copilot license. You need to build a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent that answers employee questions based on your company’s HR policy documents. What should you add to the solution?
options: a capability
instructions
a knowledge source
a starter prompt

questions.question: You need to use Microsoft 365 Copilot to summarize a document stored on a Microsoft SharePoint Online site. You are NOT authorized to access the document. What occurs when you prompt Copilot to summarize the document?
options: Copilot blocks the request.
Copilot prompts you to request elevated access.
Copilot summarizes the document and provides a citation.
Copilot summarizes the document by using Microsoft Graph.
questions.note: Objective: 1.1 Understand generative AI capabilities across Microsoft 365 experiences What This Item Tests: Understand how Copilot works to keep your organization’s information private and secure Additional Reading: User access and data privacy How does Microsoft 365 Copilot protect organizational data? Security for Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Copilot enforces the same access controls as Microsoft 365 and cannot generate responses from content the user is not authorized to access, so the request is blocked. Copilot does not request or grant elevated permissions, does not bypass access controls by using Microsoft Graph, and does not summarize or cite content that the user cannot access.

questions.question: You need to delete a memory that you previously saved in Microsoft 365 Copilot. Which Copilot setting should you use?
options: Chat history
Custom instructions
Enhanced personalization
Saved memories
questions.note: Objective: 3.2 Manage meetings and collaboration What This Item Tests: Describe how Copilot uses memory and instructions Additional Reading: Manage Copilot Memory in Microsoft 365 Copilot Rationale: Saved memories is the Copilot setting that enables users to view and delete information they have saved explicitly. Chat history and Custom instructions are managed separately, and Enhanced personalization controls behavior rather than individual saved memories.

questions.question: You use Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat throughout the week to draft emails and summarize documents. You need to reopen a conversation you created earlier in the week. Where can you access your previous Copilot Chat conversations?
options: the chat history
Copilot Notebooks
the Copilot settings
a new chat
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Understand how to find previous conversations Additional Reading: Revisit your Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat history Rationale: The chat history displays previous Copilot Chat conversations and enables you to reopen them. Copilot Notebooks is used to organize and work with selected content but does not function as the primary chat history view. The Copilot settings manage configuration options and do not provide access to prior conversations. Starting a new chat creates a new conversation rather than reopening an existing one.

questions.question: You use Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat and add several conversations to notebook for an ongoing project. One conversation is no longer needed. You need to remove the conversation completely from Copilot Chat. What should you do?
options: Start a new chat.
Rename the conversation.
Remove the conversation from the notebook.
Delete the conversation from the chat history.
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Delete a chat Additional Reading: Revisit your Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat history Rationale: Deleting the conversation from the chat history removes it from the visible Copilot Chat conversations. Removing a conversation from a notebook only disconnects it from that notebook but does not delete it from the chat history. Renaming the conversation changes its title but does not remove it. Starting a new chat creates a separate conversation and does not affect existing ones.

questions.question: You are configuring instructions for a Microsoft 365 Copilot agent that summarizes weekly project updates for managers. You need the responses to be consistent, concise, and focused on key information. What should you configure for the agent instructions?
options: Write a professional summary of the weekly updates.
Summarize weekly project updates in three sections and include as much detail as possible.
Summarize weekly project updates in three labeled sections and limit the response to 150 words.
Provide a detailed summary of each update and include all relevant information without limiting the length.
questions.note: Objective: 2.3 Create and manage Microsoft 365 Copilot agents What This Item Tests: Configure agent settings such as instructions, capabilities and suggested prompts Additional Reading: Write effective instructions for declarative agents Rationale: The most effective instruction clearly defines structure and includes a measurable length constraint, ensuring consistent and concise responses. The option that includes “as much detail as possible” conflicts with the goal of conciseness. The detailed summary without length limits contradicts the requirement for concise output. The professional summary option is too vague and lacks specific formatting or constraints.

questions.question: Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence. In Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, a notebook is used to [Target_A].
options: group related conversations and materials together
organize archived conversations into folders in the chat history
search across previous conversations
store deleted conversations
questions.note: Objective: 2.2 Manage conversations in Copilot What This Item Tests: Add a conversation to a notebook Additional Reading: Get started with Microsoft 365 Copilot Notebooks Rationale: A notebook is used to group related conversations and materials together, so that users can continue working on a topic in context. It does not create folders within the chat history and does not function as the primary search tool for conversations. Deleted conversations are not stored in notebooks.
